headline: How FITB's NYSE Move Reflects Its Transition Into a Larger U.S. Bank
summary: Fifth Third is moving from Nasdaq to NYSE after its Comerica merger, signaling a larger national banking push and aim to lift institutional visibility and scale.

headline: Fifth Third's SmartShield® Helps Customers Fight Bank Impersonation Scams
summary: CINCINNATI, June 02, 2026--Fifth Third (NASDAQ: FITB) today announced the launch of a new in-app feature, Report Phishing, that allows customers to quickly identify and report bank impersonation scams, one of the fastest growing forms of financial crime. Available within the SmartShield® experience in the Fifth Third mobile app, the tool lets customers submit suspicious texts, emails, or social media messages and receive confirmation on whether they are fraudulent. headline: Fifth Third Shifts To NYSE As Capital Structure And Valuation Draw Focus
summary: Fifth Third Bancorp is shifting its stock listing from Nasdaq to the New York Stock Exchange. All publicly traded securities of the company are scheduled to move exchanges in mid June. For investors tracking NasdaqGS:FITB, this listing transfer comes with the stock recently closing at $48.56. Over the past year, the share price is up 32.1%, and over the past three years the stock has gained 114.5%.
